items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: A fresh 40 year yen low
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh is speaking on Wednesday Canada, Mexico push to save USMCA as US holds out for changes Korea exports surge on AI chip boom as shares slide on profit-taking RatingDog PMI caps China's strongest quarter for manufacturing since 2020 China S&P Global/Rating Dog Manufacturing PMI, June 2026: 51.7 (expected 51.6, prior 51.8) Yen hits 40-year low as Treasury yields drive dollar higher Australia May 2026 Building Permits -1.1% m/m (expected 1.0%, prior –3.4%) PBOC sets USD/ CNY mid-point today at 6.8067 (vs. estimate at 6.7795) MUFG: Japan's FX warnings fall short of signalling imminent yen intervention Japan June Manufacturing PMI (final) 54.8 (vs. preliminary 54.9, prior 54.5) Japan firms lift inflation expectations as Tankan sentiment beats forecasts WSJ: Trump weighs all-out war on Iran but opts to stick with talks Confirmed - Lutnick announces Anthropic Fable 5 approved RBNZ preview: Westpac see July 8 rate hold. Tightening cycle still in effect, pared back Australia Manufacturing PMI (final) 51.5 (flash reading 51.2, prior 50.7). 5 month high. ICYMI: BoE's Bailey says won't rush to raise rates on oil-driven inflation spike Politico reports that US is expected to remove the export ban on Fable 'tonight' Oil shock torpedoes NZ recovery, sends inflation surging: IMF Trump's 2025 filing shows crypto paid off in a big way, more than $1bn in his pocket Oil: Private inventory survey shows a headline crude oil draw greater than expected Major US indices close the quarter with solid gains US VP Vance says ready to drop bombs on Iran again investingLive Americas market news wrap: JOLTS boosts optimism on jobs Summary: Vance says Trump ready to resume Iran strikes if talks fail, per social media citing Al Jazeera; WSJ later reported Trump has been briefed on full-scale war options but is sticking with diplomacy for now, tolerant of the August 18 deadline slipping Oil rangebound, gold slips back under US$4,000 BoJ Tankan shows big manufacturer sentiment at +22, its best since 2018, and big non-manufacturer sentiment at +37, its best since 1991, both beating forecasts; inflation expectations also crept higher Japan's manufacturing PMI hit 54.8, best quarterly performance since 2014 Yen extends slide to a fresh 40-year low above 162.75, as the broader dollar firms on higher yields China's RatingDog PMI eased to 51.7 but capped the sector's strongest quarter since 2020 Trump's financial disclosure shows over $1 billion in combined crypto income, largely from his $TRUMP coin Nike beat estimates but flagged a tougher consumer backdrop, shares fell US Commerce Department lifts export controls on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos AI models, weeks after ordering their suspension over national security concerns Geopolitics and oil The Iran story took a sharper edge today. Vance was reported, via social media citing Al Jazeera, as saying Trump is ready to resume strikes, framing Washington's choice as either a long-term deal conditioned on Iran changing its behaviour or simply locking in the gains already made. He said the US wants permanent, verifiable commitments on denuclearisation, backed by ongoing inspections rather than a one-off declaration. That was followed later by a Wall Street Journal report offering some reassurance: Trump has been briefed on options for a full return to war but has chosen, for now, to stick with talks, and is comfortable letting negotiations run past the August 18 deadline for a nuclear deal. Despite the war-risk headlines, oil stayed rangebound through the session, while gold slipped back under US$4,000. Japan A busy day out of Tokyo. The BoJ's Tankan showed corporate sentiment running well ahead of expectations, with the big manufacturer index at +22, its strongest since 2018, and big non-manufacturers at +37, the best reading since 1991. Inflation expectations also nudged higher across every time horizon, reinforcing the case that price pressures are becoming more entrenched. That was reinforced by the manufacturing PMI, which rose to 54.8, capping Japan's best quarterly performance since 2014, powered by the fastest new order growth since January 2022. None of that strength has helped the yen, though, which extended its slide to a fresh 40-year low above 162.75 against the dollar, with the broader dollar also firmer on the back of higher yields, even if moves stayed within limited ranges. China China's RatingDog manufacturing PMI eased slightly to 51.7 in June, but the softer headline print still capped the sector's strongest quarter since 2020, with input cost inflation cooling and hiring picking up pace. Company and policy news Nike's fiscal Q4 and full-year numbers beat Wall Street estimates on paper, but a soft revenue picture and cautious guidance sent shares lower. Management pointed to a more complex macro backdrop weighing on store traffic and discretionary spending globally, with a deceleration in North American retail sales trends emerging by mid-April as consumers came under pressure. Trump's own financial disclosure landed too, showing more than $1 billion in combined crypto income, the bulk of it tied to his $TRUMP meme coin. And in tech policy, the US Commerce Department lifted export controls on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos AI models, just under three weeks after ordering the company to suspend access over national security concerns. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美联储主席沃什将于周三发表讲话；加拿大和墨西哥力图挽救《美墨加协定》（USMCA），而美国坚持要求进行修改；受人工智能芯片热潮推动，韩国出口激增，但股市因获利回吐而下跌；RatingDog制造业PMI数据显示，中国制造业迎来2020年以来表现最强劲的一个季度；中国标普全球/RatingDog制造业PMI，2026年6月：51.7（预期51.6，前值51.8） 美债收益率推高美元，日元跌至40年低点 澳大利亚2026年5月建筑许可环比下降1.1%（预期1.0%，前值-3.4%） 中国人民银行今日将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8067（市场预期为6.7795） 三菱日联金融集团（MUFG）： 日本外汇警告尚未表明将立即干预日元 日本6月制造业PMI（终值）54.8（初值为54.9，前值为54.5） 随着短观调查结果好于预期，日本企业上调通胀预期 《华尔街日报》： 特朗普曾考虑对伊朗发动全面战争，但最终选择继续谈判 已确认——卢特尼克宣布Anthropic Fable 5获批 新西兰联储前瞻：西太平洋银行预计7月8日将维持利率不变。紧缩周期仍在持续，但力度有所收窄 澳大利亚制造业PMI（终值）51.5（初值51.2，前值50.7）。创5个月新高。 【错过了吗】：英国央行行长贝利表示，面对受油价推动的通胀飙升，不会仓促加息 《Politico》报道称，美国预计将于“今晚”解除对《Fable》的出口禁令 油价冲击重创新西兰经济复苏，导致通胀飙升：国际货币基金组织 特朗普2025年纳税申报表显示加密货币投资大获回报，其口袋里多出逾10亿美元 原油： 私人库存调查显示，原油总库存降幅超出预期 美国主要股指本季度收盘录得强劲涨幅 美国副总统万斯表示准备再次对伊朗发动空袭 investingLive 美洲市场新闻综述：JOLTS报告提振就业乐观情绪 摘要：据社交媒体援引半岛电视台报道，万斯称若谈判失败，特朗普准备重启对伊朗的打击； 《华尔街日报》随后报道称，特朗普已听取了全面战争方案的简报，但目前仍坚持外交路线，对8月18日的最后期限推迟持容忍态度 原油价格区间震荡，黄金回落至4,000美元下方 日本央行短观调查显示，制造业景气指数为+22，创2018年以来新高；非制造业景气指数为+37，创1991年以来新高，均好于预期； 通胀预期也小幅上升；日本制造业PMI升至54.8，创2014年以来最佳季度表现；日元延续跌势，跌破162.7关口，创下40年来新低。 5，随着收益率上升推动美元整体走强，中国RatingDog采购经理人指数（PMI）降至51.7，但该行业仍创下自2020年以来最强劲的季度表现；特朗普的财务披露显示，其加密货币收入总额超过10亿美元， 其中大部分来自其发行的$TRUMP代币；耐克业绩超出预期，但警告消费环境将趋紧，股价应声下跌；美国商务部解除对Anthropic公司Fable和Mythos人工智能模型的出口管制，此前数周因国家安全担忧曾下令暂停相关出口；地缘政治与石油：今日伊朗局势趋于紧张。据社交媒体援引半岛电视台报道，万斯表示特朗普已准备好重启空袭，并将华盛顿的选择描述为：要么达成以伊朗改变行为为条件的长期协议，要么仅仅锁定已取得的成果。他表示，美国希望在无核化方面获得永久且可核查的承诺，并辅以持续的核查，而非一次性的声明。 随后，《华尔街日报》的一篇报道带来了一些安抚性消息：特朗普已听取了全面重启战争的各种方案的简报，但目前选择继续坚持谈判，并且不介意让谈判持续到8月18日核协议截止日期之后。尽管媒体头条充斥着战争风险的报道，但原油价格在整个交易时段内维持区间震荡，而黄金价格则回落至4,000美元以下。日本 东京市场今日交易繁忙。日本央行发布的“短观”调查显示，企业信心远超预期：大型制造业指数为+22，创2018年以来新高；大型非制造业指数为+37，创1991年以来最佳读数。各时间周期的通胀预期也小幅上升，进一步印证了价格压力正日益固化。这一趋势还得到了制造业PMI的佐证——该指数升至54.8，创下日本自2014年以来最佳季度表现，主要得益于新订单增速达到2022年1月以来的最高水平。然而，这些强劲表现并未提振日元，日元兑美元汇率延续跌势，跌至162.75以上的新低，创下40年来新低。受收益率上升推动，美元指数整体走强，尽管波动幅度仍有限。 中国 6月份，中国RatingDog制造业PMI小幅回落至51.7，但尽管这一总指数数据有所走软，该行业仍创下自2020年以来表现最强劲的一个季度，其中投入成本通胀有所缓解，招聘步伐有所加快。企业与政策动态 耐克财年第四季度及全年业绩虽从表面上看超出华尔街预期，但疲软的营收表现和谨慎的业绩指引导致股价下跌。管理层指出，更复杂的宏观环境正拖累全球门店客流量和可自由支配消费，随着消费者面临压力，北美零售销售趋势自4月中旬起已显现放缓迹象。特朗普本人的财务披露报告也同步公布，显示其加密货币收入合计超过10亿美元，其中大部分与他的$TRUMP迷因币相关。在科技政策方面，美国商务部解除了对Anthropic公司Fable和Mythos人工智能模型的出口管制，此前不到三周，该部门曾因国家安全担忧要求该公司暂停相关访问权限。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。

items.title: investingLive Americas market news wrap: JOLTS boosts optimism on jobs
items.summary: JOLTs job openings 7.594M vs 7.300M estimate US June consumer confidence 91.2 vs 94.8 expected Canada April GDP +0.5% vs +0.4% expected US April CaseShiller 20-city house price index +1.1% y/y vs +0.9% expected FHFA monthly home price index for April -0.1% versus a revised 0.2% last month Iran's Ghalibaf: We won't enter any more negotiations until MOU conditions are met ECB debating doubling banks' minimum reserve requirement to 2% from 1% - report BOE's Bailey: We have time to judge the pass through of higher energy prices Fed's Hammack: The jobs market is right around full employment, growth looks good US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, striking down Trump order Oman proposes a fee plan for the Strait of Hormuz -- report Germany June preliminary CPI +2.3% vs +2.6% y/y expected The top-25 stocks in the S&P 500 so far this year are dominated by chip and AI names Markets: Gold flat at $4016 US 10-year yields up 6.2 bps to 4.44% WTI crude oil down 72-cents to $70.02 S&P 500 up 0.9% AUD leads, JPY lags The yen bulls capitulated on Tuesday as the currency continued to carve out 40-year lows in an accelerating fashion. The pair did most of its damage early in the day as it rose as high as 162.66 in a steady climb through the London fix. The caveat on the move is that it came on the final day of the quarter and that could be skewing things. There was a fundamental backing though as the JOLTS report showed job openings at a two year high and boosted optimism for this week's non-farm payrolls report. Elsewhere, the US dollar was generally softer with commodity currencies making gains. Optimism about global growth is creeping up and that's put a small bid into the antipodeans. Talk is shifting towards the wage gains in the US jobs report as a possible catalyst towards a rate hike in July or September. Elsewhere, stocks were nicely bid to wrap up a sensational quarter for the AI trade. The Nasdaq led the way on strength in chip names once again. Intel, Nvidia and Texas Instruments were among the top gainers. Dispersion was thinner than you would like and decliners actually outpaced gainers. This article was written by Adam Button at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: JOLTs职位空缺数为759.4万，高于预期的730.0万；美国6月消费者信心指数为91.2，低于预期的94.8；加拿大4月GDP增长0.5%，高于预期的0.4%；美国4月Case-Shiller 20城房价指数同比上涨1.1%，高于预期的0.9%；联邦住房金融局（FHFA）4月月度房价指数 -0.1%，而上月数据经修正后为0.2% 伊朗加利巴夫：在谅解备忘录（MOU）条件得到满足之前，我们不会再进行任何谈判 据报道，欧洲央行正在讨论将银行法定准备金率从1%上调至2% 英格兰银行行长贝利：我们还有时间来评估能源价格上涨的传导效应 美联储的哈马克： 就业市场已接近充分就业，经济增长态势良好 美国最高法院维持出生公民权，推翻特朗普行政令 据报道，阿曼提议对霍尔木兹海峡征收通行费 德国6月初步CPI同比上涨2.3%，低于预期值2.6% 今年迄今标普500指数表现最佳的25只股票中，芯片和人工智能类股占据主导 市场： 黄金持平于4016美元；美国10年期国债收益率上涨6.2个基点至4.44%；WTI原油下跌72美分至70.02美元；标普500指数上涨0.9%；澳元领涨，日元垫底。周二，随着日元以加速态势持续创下40年来新低，日元多头最终投降。 该货币对的涨幅主要集中在当日早盘，在伦敦定盘时段期间稳步攀升，最高触及162.66。需要注意的是，这一走势出现在季度最后一天，这可能会对行情产生扭曲影响。不过，基本面提供了支撑：JOLTS报告显示职位空缺数达到两年高点，提振了市场对本周非农就业报告的乐观预期。其他方面，美元整体走软，大宗商品货币则有所上涨。市场对全球经济增长的乐观情绪正在悄然升温，这为澳元和纽元带来了一定买盘。市场讨论的焦点正转向美国就业报告中的工资增长，将其视为7月或9月加息的潜在催化剂。此外，股市表现强劲，为人工智能板块的惊艳季度画上了圆满句号。纳斯达克指数再次领涨，主要得益于芯片股的强劲表现。英特尔、英伟达和德州仪器均位列涨幅榜前列。不过，市场涨跌股分布不如预期均衡，下跌个股数量实际上超过了上涨个股。本文作者为investinglive.com的亚当·巴顿。

The pair did most of its damage early in the day as it rose as high as 162.66 in a steady climb through the London fix. The caveat on the move is that it came on the final day of the quarter and that could be skewing things. There was a fundamental backing though as the JOLTS report showed job openings at a two year high and boosted optimism for this week's non-farm payrolls report. Elsewhere, the US dollar was generally softer with commodity currencies making gains. Optimism about global growth is creeping up and that's put a small bid into the antipodeans. Talk is shifting towards the wage gains in the US jobs report as a possible catalyst towards a rate hike in July or September. This article was written by Adam Button at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 关键宏观数据超预期时，点差与滑点风险通常放大，建议降低仓位并控制杠杆。
items.impactEn: When macro data surprises, spread/slippage risk often widens; consider smaller position size and tighter leverage control.

items.title: The best and worst performers in the first half of 2026
items.summary: The mood music at the start of the year is certainly very much different to what it is now as we approach the second half of the year. In January, everyone and anyone was raving about precious metals and we looked on course for more Fed rate cuts with US president Trump set to get his way in replacing Powell at the helm. Besides that, the AI trade was in full swing more or less. There were some minor concerns in late 2025 but big tech showed much resilience overall up until last month. Fast forward to today, what a completely different landscape it is for markets. So, who were the best performers in the first half of the year? The big standout is of course South Korea's KOSPI index. There are no superlatives for 100% gains in six months, effectively doubling in value. The AI trade is the key driver in all of this, with a historic surge in memory-chip heavyweights like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. Even after the challenges in June, it's been quite the parabolic rise to the top for South Korea's main benchmark index. The tech and semiconductor surge this year also helped to see Taiwan's TAIEX index surge by 59% and Japan's Nikkei 225 index also surging by over 39% in the first half of 2026. The global thirst for AI server architecture and hardware helped a lot with the former, with Taiwan's main benchmark index driven by TSMC especially, which gained by over 55% in the first six months of the year. As for the Nikkei, the surging rally in tech definitely helped but also a freefalling Japanese yen currency. The massive decline in the yen has also made shares of Japanese exporters look extraordinarily lucrative to foreign buyers. And despite an immense drop in the past two months, oil prices are still one of the better performers so far this year. WTI crude saw over 20% gains in the first six months of 2026, in spite of a sharp drop of over 40% since the March peak. We all know the story here with the US-Iran conflict definitely changing the outlook for the oil market, which looked set for a massive supply glut before this. Besides the above, US stocks also fared well as big tech rode the wave of earnings momentum to outperform in the first half of 2026. The Nasdaq posted 13% gains while the S&P 500 locked in 9.5% gains in posting another resilient and strong showing so far this year. That being said, it was a rather turbulent last one month for big tech especially. While Nvidia managed to still hold up, the rest of the Magnificent Seven suffered to post their worst collective month on record in June. Hyperscalers are being punished as investors are in the show me the money phase in demanding results to back the tens of billions on AI capital expenditures. Other than that, the first half of the year was also kind to the likes of the US dollar, Australian dollar, and copper. So, what about the worst performers in the first six months of 2026? The most notable one is arguably Bitcoin, which fell by some 33% in a plunge back below $60,000. In June, there was a record-breaking $4.06 billion in net outflows from US spot ETFs alone. And that certainly did not help with the crypto market sentiment, which was already down in the dumps for the better part of the year. One major argument point is perhaps cryptocurrencies are giving way for a meteoric rise in another potential asset class. From before: Of cryptocurrencies and collectibles Then, we also have precious metals being among the worst performers too. What a difference five months can make, eh? Gold itself is down nearly 8% in the first half of the year with silver down by roughly 19%. And things look like they will get worse before getting any better for precious metals at this point. The US-Iran conflict knocked down leveraged trades and with a more hawkish Fed outlook now, it is creating some big headwinds for gold and silver to go up against. That especially after the already sharp technical correction in early February. Besides that, the likes of platinum and palladium are also suffering with the former being down by 24% and the latter down by 25% in the first half of the year. In terms of major currencies, the Japanese yen is of course the standout with USD/JPY now rising to a 40-year high. And that comes despite Tokyo officials intervening in late April to try and stop the bleeding. While USD/JPY only saw 3.6% gains in the first half of the year, it is best to be reminded that the currency pair is up roughly 10% since the start of October last year when Japan prime minister Takaichi took office. Other than that, "sell Indonesia" was the other main theme sweeping across desks globally amid a capital flight out of the country. Domestic politics have never been messier and investors have lost almost all confidence over president Prabowo Subianto's aggressive moves to consolidate political power. That all eventually led to MSCI freezing Indonesia's stocks in its indexes and threatened a downgrade to "Frontier" status. The Jakarta Composite index ended the first half of the year down some 34% with the Indonesian rupiah currency also taking the crown as the worst performing Asian currency (and arguably the worst emerging market currency) so far this year. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 年初的市场氛围与如今临近下半年时的氛围确实大相径庭。1月份，所有人都对贵金属赞不绝口，当时美联储似乎正朝着进一步降息的方向发展，美国总统特朗普也即将如愿以偿，让鲍威尔卸任并由他人接任美联储主席一职。此外，人工智能相关股票的交易也大致处于如火如荼的状态。2025年底虽曾出现一些小幅担忧，但直到上个月，科技巨头们总体上都展现出了极强的韧性。快进到今天，市场格局已截然不同。那么，今年上半年表现最佳的究竟是谁呢？最引人注目的当属韩国KOSPI指数。六个月内涨幅达100%，市值实际上翻了一番，这简直无法用任何词汇来形容。人工智能概念股是这一切的关键驱动力，三星电子和SK海力士等内存芯片巨头股价创下了历史性飙升。即便经历了6月份的挑战，韩国这一主要基准指数仍呈现出抛物线式的飙升态势，一路攀升至顶峰。 今年科技和半导体行业的强劲表现，也推动台湾加权指数在2026年上半年飙升59%，日本日经225指数同样上涨了39%以上。全球对人工智能服务器架构和硬件的强烈需求对前者起到了很大推动作用，其中台湾的主要基准指数尤其受到台积电的带动，该公司在2026年上半年涨幅超过55%。至于日经指数，科技股的强劲上涨固然起到了推动作用，但日元汇率的暴跌也是重要因素。日元的大幅贬值也使得日本出口企业的股票在外国投资者眼中显得格外具有吸引力。尽管过去两个月油价大幅下跌，但今年迄今为止，油价仍是表现较好的资产之一。尽管自3月高点以来暴跌了40%以上，但2026年上半年，西德克萨斯中质原油（WTI）仍上涨了20%以上。众所周知，美伊冲突彻底改变了原油市场的前景——在此之前，该市场原本似乎将面临巨大的供应过剩。除上述因素外，美国股市表现同样强劲，科技巨头借助盈利动能的东风，在2026年上半年表现优于大盘。 纳斯达克指数上涨13%，标普500指数则录得9.5%的涨幅，今年迄今再次展现出强劲的韧性。话虽如此，过去一个月对科技巨头而言却颇为动荡。尽管英伟达（Nvidia）仍能保持坚挺，但其余“七大科技巨头”却表现惨淡，6月创下了有史以来最差的月度集体表现。随着投资者进入“用业绩说话”的阶段，要求企业拿出实际成果来支撑数百亿美元的人工智能资本支出，超大规模科技公司正因此受到市场惩罚。除此之外，今年上半年对美元、澳元和铜等资产也较为有利。那么，2026年上半年表现最差的资产又有哪些呢？最引人注目的当属比特币，其价格暴跌约33%，跌回6万美元以下。仅6月份，美国现货ETF的净赎回额就创下40.6亿美元的纪录。这无疑进一步打击了加密货币市场的情绪——而该市场在今年的大部分时间里本就已陷入低迷。一个主要的争论点或许在于，加密货币正在为另一类潜在资产类别的火箭式上涨让路。 前文回顾：关于加密货币和收藏品 此外，贵金属的表现也同样垫底。短短五个月竟能带来如此大的变化，不是吗？今年上半年，黄金价格下跌了近8%，白银则下跌了约19%。目前看来，贵金属市场在好转之前，情况可能会进一步恶化。美伊冲突打压了杠杆交易，加之美联储目前采取更鹰派的立场，这给金银价格的上涨带来了巨大的阻力。尤其是在2月初已经经历过一次剧烈的技术性回调之后。此外，铂金和钯金等贵金属也遭受重创，前者上半年下跌24%，后者下跌25%。就主要货币而言，日元无疑表现最为突出，美元兑日元汇率目前已升至40年高点。尽管东京当局在4月下旬曾出手干预，试图止住跌势，但美元兑日元汇率仍持续走高。虽然该汇率上半年仅上涨了3.6%，但值得注意的是，自去年10月初日本首相高市早苗上任以来，该汇率已累计上涨约10%。 除此之外，随着资本从印尼外流，“抛售印尼”成为席卷全球交易台的另一大主题。国内政治局势从未如此混乱，投资者对总统普拉博沃·苏比安托为巩固政治权力而采取的激进举措几乎丧失了全部信心。这一切最终导致MSCI将其指数中的印尼股票冻结，并威胁将其评级下调至“前沿市场”级别。雅加达综合指数上半年收跌约34%，印尼卢比也成为今年迄今为止表现最差的亚洲货币（甚至可以说是表现最差的新兴市场货币）。本文作者为investinglive.com的贾斯汀·洛（Justin Low）。
items.summaryEn: The mood music at the start of the year is certainly very much different to what it is now as we approach the second half of the year. In January, everyone and anyone was raving about precious metals and we looked on course for more Fed rate cuts with US president Trump set to get his way in replacing Powell at the helm. Besides that, the AI trade was in full swing more or less. There were some minor concerns in late 2025 but big tech showed much resilience overall up until last month. Fast forward to today, what a completely different landscape it is for markets. So, who were the best performers in the first half of the year? The big standout is of course South Korea's KOSPI index. There are no superlatives for 100% gains in six months, effectively doubling in value. The AI trade is the key driver in all of this, with a historic surge in memory-chip heavyweights like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. Even after the challenges in June, it's been quite the parabolic rise to the top for South Korea's main benchmark index. The tech and semiconductor surge this year also helped to see Taiwan's TAIEX index surge by 59% and Japan's Nikkei 225 index also surging by over 39% in the first half of 2026. The global thirst for AI server architecture and hardware helped a lot with the former, with Taiwan's main benchmark index driven by TSMC especially, which gained by over 55% in the first six months of the year. As for the Nikkei, the surging rally in tech definitely helped but also a freefalling Japanese yen currency. The massive decline in the yen has also made shares of Japanese exporters look extraordinarily lucrative to foreign buyers. And despite an immense drop in the past two months, oil prices are still one of the better performers so far this year. WTI crude saw over 20% gains in the first six months of 2026, in spite of a sharp drop of over 40% since the March peak. We all know the story here with the US-Iran conflict definitely changing the outlook for the oil market, which looked set for a massive supply glut before this. Besides the above, US stocks also fared well as big tech rode the wave of earnings momentum to outperform in the first half of 2026. The Nasdaq posted 13% gains while the S&P 500 locked in 9.5% gains in posting another resilient and strong showing so far this year. That being said, it was a rather turbulent last one month for big tech especially. While Nvidia managed to still hold up, the rest of the Magnificent Seven suffered to post their worst collective month on record in June. Hyperscalers are being punished as investors are in the show me the money phase in demanding results to back the tens of billions on AI capital expenditures. Other than that, the first half of the year was also kind to the likes of the US dollar, Australian dollar, and copper. So, what about the worst performers in the first six months of 2026? The most notable one is arguably Bitcoin, which fell by some 33% in a plunge back below $60,000. In June, there was a record-breaking $4.06 billion in net outflows from US spot ETFs alone. And that certainly did not help with the crypto market sentiment, which was already down in the dumps for the better part of the year. One major argument point is perhaps cryptocurrencies are giving way for a meteoric rise in another potential asset class. From before: Of cryptocurrencies and collectibles Then, we also have precious metals being among the worst performers too. What a difference five months can make, eh? Gold itself is down nearly 8% in the first half of the year with silver down by roughly 19%. And things look like they will get worse before getting any better for precious metals at this point. The US-Iran conflict knocked down leveraged trades and with a more hawkish Fed outlook now, it is creating some big headwinds for gold and silver to go up against. That especially after the already sharp technical correction in early February. Besides that, the likes of platinum and palladium are also suffering with the former being down by 24% and the latter down by 25% in the first half of the year. In terms of major currencies, the Japanese yen is of course the standout with USD/JPY now rising to a 40-year high. And that comes despite Tokyo officials intervening in late April to try and stop the bleeding. While USD/JPY only saw 3.6% gains in the first half of the year, it is best to be reminded that the currency pair is up roughly 10% since the start of October last year when Japan prime minister Takaichi took office. Other than that, "sell Indonesia" was the other main theme sweeping across desks globally amid a capital flight out of the country. Domestic politics have never been messier and investors have lost almost all confidence over president Prabowo Subianto's aggressive moves to consolidate political power. That all eventually led to MSCI freezing Indonesia's stocks in its indexes and threatened a downgrade to "Frontier" status. The Jakarta Composite index ended the first half of the year down some 34% with the Indonesian rupiah currency also taking the crown as the worst performing Asian currency (and arguably the worst emerging market currency) so far this year. This article was written by Justin Low at investinglive.com.
